theory.canonical_statement: In the coupled phonon--magnon system, the total helicity H\_total = H\_acoustic + H\_magnetic is conserved.
theory.technical_interpretation: In the coupled phonon--magnon system, the total helicity H\_total = H\_acoustic + H\_magnetic is conserved.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: The coupled phonon--magnon system carries a total helicity composed of acoustic and magnetic contributions.
Under the stated conservation law, exchange in one channel is balanced by the other.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\paragraph{Primary Equation.}
\[
H_{\mathrm{total}}=H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}+H_{\mathrm{magnetic}},\qquad \partial_t H_{\mathrm{total}}=0.
\]
\paragraph{Conservation Derivation.}
Differentiate the total-helicity decomposition with respect to time:
\[
\partial_t H_{\mathrm{total}}=\partial_t H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}+\partial_t H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}.
\]
Applying the stated conservation constraint gives
\[
0=\partial_t H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}+\partial_t H_{\mathrm{magnetic}},\qquad \partial_t H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}=-\partial_t H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}.
\]
\paragraph{Interpretation.}
Within the coupled model, helicity can be transferred between acoustic and magnetic channels while the total remains invariant.
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER

review_source_enrichment.method: equation-aligned PDF evidence followed by manual review draft

symbol_definitions.H_{\\mathrm{total}}: total helicity conserved in the coupled phonon--magnon system

dictionary_sections.definition: A helicity transfer constraint is a conservation law for a coupled phonon--magnon system. It states that acoustic and magnetic contributions may exchange helicity while their combined total remains fixed.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In this entry helicity measures twistedness or linking carried by the coupled channels. A change in the acoustic portion is therefore paired with an opposing change in the magnetic portion, rather than interpreted as new helicity being created by the interaction.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: Differentiating the sum gives \(\partial_t H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}=-\partial_t H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}\) under the conservation condition. The sourced text further identifies controlled opposite-helicity acoustic vortices as a route for testing compensating magnetic-helicity change.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The aligned source names this entry as a conservation law, gives the sum \(H_{\mathrm{total}}=H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}+H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}\), and states that coupling transfers helicity between sound and magnetic fields without creating or destroying it.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The equation is an accounting constraint on internal transfer, not by itself an energy-conversion efficiency law. It fixes an invariant quantity against which a phonon--magnon interaction model can be checked.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: \(H_{\mathrm{total}}\) is total helicity of the coupled system; \(H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}\) is the acoustic-channel contribution; and \(H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}\) is the magnetic-channel contribution. The derivative equality expresses the stated conservation.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry carries the tertiary arc from coupling mechanisms to a topological invariant: exchange may occur, but the conserved combined helicity restricts admissible transfer histories.

tzp_thesaurus.meaning: A measure of twistedness or linking carried by a field configuration.
Redistribution of helicity between acoustic and magnetic channels under coupling.
A relation requiring the combined helicity to remain invariant during transfer.

review_publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: The conserved-sum relation makes any acoustic helicity transfer compensating: within the coupled model, its time variation is balanced by the magnetic helicity variation.
review_publication_overrides.proof_latex:
\textbf{Statement:} Within the stated coupled system,
\[
H_{\mathrm{total}}=H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}+H_{\mathrm{magnetic}},\qquad \partial_t H_{\mathrm{total}}=0.
\]
\textbf{Logic Validation:}\\
\textbf{Proposition:} Conservation of total helicity entails balanced transfer between the acoustic and magnetic channels.\\
\textbf{Proof:} Differentiation of the additive decomposition yields \(\partial_t H_{\mathrm{total}}=\partial_t H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}+\partial_t H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}\). Substitution of the sourced conservation condition gives \(\partial_t H_{\mathrm{acoustic}}=-\partial_t H_{\mathrm{magnetic}}\).\\
\textbf{VERDICT:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ \textbf{TRUE} --- formal conservation consequence under the stated model\\
\textbf{Formal Status:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ THEORETICAL -- source-backed CHL obligation\\
\textbf{Physical Status:} THEORETICAL -- empirical realization remains model-dependent
